entry.title: Re: ABAP development tools for Visual Studio Code: Your Questions Answered
entry.content:
As much as I like the this VSCode initiative, I'm really disappointed about question 2:

"

Will ADT for Visual Studio Code replace ADT for Eclipse?

No. ADT for Visual Studio Code is an additional option, not a replacement.

"

That means that we will have 3 dev tools and (currently) at least 2 need to be used already in parallel:

a) SAP GUI (because Eclipse cannot do everything)

b) Eclipse (because SAP GUI cannot do everything, e.g. CDS, and VSCode also cannot do many things yet)

c) VSCode (and if you now introduce some functionality which only works there, it's really mandatory to work with 3 tools... that would be crazy)

Why can't you enable full feature parity EVERYWHERE and leave the choice to the developers? Already going in with the target of NOT implementing everything is already admitting failure.

entry.title: Inconsistent Curriculum Assignment Dates Across LMS Views
entry.content:
Hello SAP SuccessFactors Learning Community,

We recently identified an inconsistency in how Curriculum Assignment Dates are displayed across different areas of SAP SuccessFactors Learning.

For the same curriculum assignment, the displayed assignment date may differ between:

  • Admin > Curricula tab
  • Admin > Assigned Items tab
  • Learner view / Learning Plan
  • Reports

SAP confirmed this is currently “expected behavior” related to UTC storage and time zone conversion logic (KBA 3609787). However, from a business and user perspective, this creates confusion because administrators and learners may see different assignment dates for the exact same assignment event.

To address this, I submitted an SAP Customer Influence request asking SAP to provide a more consistent assignment date display approach across LMS.

If your organization has experienced similar confusion or reporting inconsistencies, please consider reviewing and voting for the enhancement request below:

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/375389

Thank you for your support and feedback.

Serguei,

entry.title: Big News: What’s New in the SAP for Me Services & Support Dashboard (June 3 Release)
entry.content:
🚀Big News: What’s New in the SAP for Me Services & Support Dashboard (June 3 Release)

We’re excited to share a major milestone in the evolution of the SAP for Me Services & Support Dashboard! Following the SAP Sapphire launch, the June 3rd update brings a wave of enhancements designed to simplify navigation, accelerate access to insights, and deliver a more intelligent, streamlined user experience.

Let’s dive into what’s new—and why it matters.

✨ A Smarter Experience with Joule

One of the most exciting changes is the continued rollout of Joule in SAP for Me.

  • The traditional knowledge search bar evolves into an AI-powered intent-driven experience
  • The central search transforms into a magnifying glass icon, encouraging users to engage with Joule
  • Functional search remains available, but AI becomes the primary entry point

Current Knowledge Search Bar

New Joule Bar

👉 This marks a clear shift toward a more intuitive, conversational way to find answers and get things done.

🔄 A Simplified Services & Support Engagements Experience

We’ve reimagined how Services & Support engagements are organized to make them clearer and easier to navigate.

Key highlights:

  • “Pre-Packaged Services” moved from Support Engagements to the Services tab
  • A simplified Support Engagements tab with a Service & Support Entitlements card
  • New Quick Access card:
    • SAP Enterprise Support Academy
    • Sub-Processor
    • Sub-Processor Insights
    • Request Extended Maintenance
    • Customer Center of Expertise (Customer COE)
    • Support Offerings

Updated Services Tab

Updated Support Engagements Tab

👉The result: faster access to the right services, with less clutter and clearer entry points.

🧭 Tab Structure Improvements for a Cleaner Journey

We’ve streamlined the dashboard experience by reorganizing and removing unnecessary elements.

What’s changed:

  • ✔️“More Resources” tab removed entirely
    - Product Availability Matrix → moved to Portfolio & Products
    - Security & Trust Service Center → moved to Finance & Legal

  • ✔️“Diagnostics, Reporting & Analytics” tab repositioned and refined
    - Renamed to “Diagnostics”
    - Focus is now early risk detection with SAP EarlyWatch Alert

  • ✔️“ALM” renamed to “Application Management” with a focus on SAP Cloud ALM
    - All cards consolidated into a central Quick Access card

Removed More Resources Tab


Updated Application Management Tab

📊Early Risk Detection with SAP EarlyWatch Alert

The Diagnostics experience gets a major boost:

  • Dedicated  system health visibility Integration card
  • Quick access to main capabilities (Solution Finder, Reports, Dashboard, Status) and forecast applications
  • One-click subscription for notifications.

New Diagnostics Tab

👉 This helps customers shift toward a more continuous and forward oriented way of managing system risks.

🧹General UX Improvements You’ll Notice Immediately

Beyond the big structural changes, we’ve made several smaller—but impactful—improvements:

  • ❌“Get Assistance” removed from all pages (now only in left-hand navigation)
  • ❌Regulatory Change Manager removed from the dashboard (relocated to Finance & Legal)
  • ✔️Cleaner left-hand navigation experience
  • ✔️Reduced duplication and clearer page purpose across the dashboard

🎯Why This Matters

It’s all about focus, simplicity, and intelligence:

  • 🔍  Less searching, more finding with Joule in SAP for Me
  • 🧭Less navigating, more doing
  • 📊 Better insights, right where you need them
  • ⚡ Faster access to your most important tools and services

🌟What’s Next?

These updates are part of a broader Services & Support Dashboard evolution (Phase 2) - with continued enhancements already planned.

We’re excited to keep building a smarter, more personalized SAP for Me experience together.

💬Other blogs you might find interesting:

  • 🎉Celebrating the Successful Launch of Joule in S... - SAP Community
  • Refining Diagnostics in SAP for Me Services & Support Dashboard

entry.title: Query on report Asset History Report (S_ALR_87011990)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

 

We are currently transferring assets within the same company code using transaction code ABUMN. As a part of the business requirement, we need to change the old cost center to new cost center for the US region. Here business is following bonus depreciation and we need a clarity while doing the asset transfer how the depreciation values to be posted to the new asset.

 

After completing the transfer and posting depreciation, we reviewed the Asset History Report (S_ALR_87011990) for depreciation areas XX with the report date of 31.05.2026. We observed the following:

 

  • Success case -For the another deactivated asset XXXXXX (Company Code XXXX, the report shows a net book value of zero.
  • Failure case - For the deactivated asset XXXXX (Company Code XXXX), the report continues to display a current net book value

entry.title: Re: EVENT_PARAMETER cuts long strings at 255 characters
entry.content: The max size of a event parameter is related to the database *server* page size. For a 4k page size, the limit is approximately 4000 bytes. Options for dealing with values that are larger than the server page size might be to break up the parameter values into chunks or perhaps staging the value in a table which is queried in the event passing the primary key of for the values row as a parameter to the event. A unique identifier might be a good option for the key.

entry.title: SAP Incentive Management: Keeping track of Payment Attributes
entry.content:
Hi,

I've been an admin for SAP Incentive Management in my company for a while now. There is a situation that I would like to discuss with this Community, that is the tracking of Payment Attributes, specifically when a recalculation of the period is required.

Let me explain; we use Generic Attributes and Numbers to store accounting values (i.e. various VAT attributes, Supplier ID, GL Account, Cost Center, etc..). After Finalize we extract Deposits and Payments through the RestAPI to generate an Invoice (self-billing) for the upstream system, I've seen a few ERP solutions by now, they all require the same; Payment becomes the Invoice Header and Deposits are used for the Invoice Lines.

Some of the attributes required for the Invoice Header are taken from the Deposits using an aggregated view; attributes like Supplier ID, Name, Start/End/Invoice Date, Legal Entity are the always same for the entire Payment but we can only store values in Generic Attributes in the Deposit.

Whenever we find ourselves in a situation where we really need to rerun a Comp & Pay Pipeline for a Finalized Period, it's all hands on deck, this is an absolute nightmare! Effectively, it's not allowed.

This is what we have to do:

(1) Secure a copy of Deposits for the target Period
(2) Rerun Comp&Pay
(3) Secure a copy of new Deposits
(4) Reset Deposits to original values (Value, GA's, GN's, GB's, GD's)
(5) Recalculate Payment and validate NO BALANCE
(6) Create manual Deposits from delta's between files 1 and 3 in open period

If you made it this far, thank you for reading along. I can almost hear you thinking "Keep Posted Results" (KPR), you are not wrong. I am a strong believer of "make the system work for you" rather then "you work for the system", that's clearly not the case here. As you can see, I mostly work with external partners but this is really no different from integrating with payroll.

My goal is to allow rerun on a Finalized Period and use the KPR data to:

(A) Be able to recreate the original Invoice (i.e. Payment + Deposits)
(B) Be able to create an invoice based on the Applied Balance (Delta between original and new Deposits)

These tables allows me to 'recreate the original Invoice' (A) but I have yet to find a solution to 'create an invoice based on the Applied Balance' (B). I'm just going to ignore releasing Held Deposits for now, to keep me from going insane.

  • CS_PAYMENT
  • CS_BALANCE
  • CS_APPLDEPOSITPAYMENTTRACE
  • CS_BALANCEPAYMENTTRACE
  • CS_APPLDEPOSITBALANCETRACE
  • CS_APPLIEDDEPOSIT
  • CS_KPRDEPOSITAPPLDEPOSITTRACE
  • CS_KPRDEPOSIT

My initial idea was to calculate some kind of delta between the original and new Deposits. I think it would probably be easier to 'simulate' (or in fact automate with a procedure / stagehook) a full reversal of the original Deposits, since the new Deposits would be calculated anyway. This would be easier to account and when aggregated leads to the same delta.

https://chatgpt.com/share/6a2066b2-fe48-8326-a522-1fcc34b914df 

I would love to hear how you (other Comp Admins) deal with these situations. And please, share this post with anyone who you think might be interested.

p.s. I found this thread: Solved: SAP Commissions: Generic Attributes for Payments - SAP Community, which does seem interesting. I doubt it solves anything, you're just moving the Attributes to a different table, one that is hidden from the UI. There are also a number of Feature Requests regarding this topic, none of which seem to be implemented any time soon.

entry.title: Re: the way i create a issue about supplier in running a...
entry.content:
Good day @KTAWA 

Thank you for your question.

 A Supplier capacity or “supplier cannot deliver enough quantity” message will not appear just because you created BOM, routing, purchase info record and work center. These objects do not define a hard supplier delivery capacity.

For supplier selection, you need to maintain Source Lists and if you want multiple suppliers to share the requirement, then maintain Quota Arrangements. Source list will allow you control valid sources for a material and plant, while quota arrangements distribute requirements between available sources.

If your gaol is to show a shortage, create demand that is higher than stock and receipts, run MRP, and check the result in Monitor Material Coverage or Manage Material Coverage. Those apps will help you analyse and resolve material coverage issues.

Here is what I recommend:

  1. Check the product master MRP settings.
  2. Maintain purchase info record for the supplier and material.
  3. Maintain source list that will control valid suppliers.                                                                                  
  4. Maintain quota arrangement if you must split demand between suppliers.       
  5. Create a demand higher than your available supply and run MRP.
  6. Check the shortage in Monitor Material Coverage.                                                            
  7. If your requirement is true supplier capacity confirmation, then you should use supplier confirmations or supplier collaboration because standard procurement does not automatically know the supplier’s physical production or delivery capacity.

Here are references for more guidance

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/49fb9b57aba49f2de10000000a44147b.html?locale=en-US 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/a565c3dddcf943c69dc8d43ac2016e4c.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/31c16f543e0de830e10000000a44538d.html?locale=en-US                    

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: PartnerEdge Build: SAP Transportation Management (TM)...
entry.content: SAP TM is embedded in SAP S/4HANA and available in on‑premise, S/4HANA Cloud private edition, and with selected scope in S/4HANA Cloud public edition. If you build your integration using SAP‑released APIs/CDS and a clean‑core, side‑by‑side approach (e.g., on SAP BTP), cloud customers can consume it as well. On‑premise‑style modifications or unreleased objects won’t run in S/4HANA Cloud public edition. For private edition, you have more flexibility, but API‑first keeps your solution portable across editions.

entry.title: Re: Logs or change history of planned orders
entry.content:
Good day @Cosmin05 

Thank you for your question.

If you need a full audit trail like, Planned order, Field Changed, Old value, New value, Changes by and Changed on, then this is normally not available from PLAF alone. In Public Cloud, you cannot solve this by reading internal tables or adding classic table logging. If the standard app and released CDS views do not expose the required history, then it is intended or you can view it as a product gap which you cand consider placing an enhancement request with SAP.

That means a full change history for planned orders is not available from table PLAF alone. PLAF reflects current order header data and can provide change related information such as timestamps, but it does not provide a complete historical log with values mentioned above. See KBA 1803240 on the existence of planned order last change timestamp field.

For the standard process, I suggest you use Manage Planned Orders to check the current planned order  and

use Application Jobs or MRP jobs log to identify whether an MRP run likely changed the planned order.

So, my recommended steps are the following:

  1. Check the planned order in Manage Planned Orders.
  2. Check the MRP run in Application Jobs.
  3. Compare the planned order change date/time with the MRP job execution time.
  4. Use Manage Material coverage to analyse the planning result.
  5. If you must avoid MRP changes, review planning time fence and firming logic.
  6. You may have to raise an improvement request with SAP if a full audit trail is mandatory.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2bba750d1e124e1ea2a039bb1cd9b6c5/e45ca7eed9f44f828768e720e8d6f7ad.html?locale=en-US

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: How to Implement SAP Business One projects successfully
entry.content:
Great breakdown on the implementation steps. Having run 20+ SMB go-lives in the Hyderabad market, I’d add one critical nuance: the go-live week is where Hyderabad SMBs lose the most money, and it’s almost never a technical issue — it’s a data readiness problem.

Three failure patterns I see repeatedly:

1. Master data validation is skipped under time pressure.

Teams migrate from Tally/legacy spreadsheets and the item master is “good enough” on cutover night. Three weeks later, inventory reports are useless because unit of measure conversions weren’t standardized. Without clean master data, you have zero real-time visibility into what’s actually happening in your warehouse.

The fix: run a parallel inventory count in the old system AND SAP B1 for 2 weeks before go-live. Any variance >2% stops go-live until resolved. This sounds painful, but it’s the single biggest predictor of whether your go-live produces reliable numbers from Day 1.

2. User training is treated as a checkbox.

SMB owners assume the consultant will “handle it.” What actually happens: 3 PowerPoint slides, a rushed demo, and a team that can log in but can’t process a goods receipt. The result: workarounds that corrupt data, followed by 6 months of cleanup.

Better model: identify 2–3 power users per department in week 1. Train them deeply. They become your go-live support army. The goal is not “everyone knows SAP” — it’s “every department has someone who can troubleshoot in real time.”

3. No cutover rollback plan.

If something breaks on go-live night, what’s the decision tree? Who decides to rollback vs. push through? Almost no SMB implementation documents this. The result: 48 hours of chaos before the inevitable rollback — which costs more than if you’d planned for it.

The go-live checklist that actually works:

  • Parallel run confirmation — both systems running simultaneously for at least 5 business days, with real-time variance tracking
  • Master data freeze — no item master, vendor, or customer changes 48 hours before cutover
  • Rollback decision criteria — documented: who decides, under what conditions, and what’s the revert process
  • Go-live support team — internal champion + consultant available for 72 hours straight
  • First-week close check — finance runs a full month-end close in SAP B1 within 5 business days of go-live
  • Batch cost reconciliation — for pharma/manufacturing: verify every component batch traces correctly through the production order to the FG batch. Gaps here become audit findings later.

The single best investment: A formal 3-day parallel run before go-live where the team processes every real transaction in BOTH systems simultaneously. Painful? Yes. Expensive? Yes. Cheaper than data recovery after a bad cutover? Far less.

For pharma and manufacturing companies in Hyderabad (especially in the Genome Valley and Patancheru clusters), the batch cost traceability piece is non-negotiable. If you’re operating under USFDA, WHO-GMP, or EU GMP compliance, your genealogy chain from raw material batches → production order → FG batch has to be complete before your first regulatory audit. Companies that skip this during implementation spend ₹15–40 lakh on remediation later.

We’ve been building AI tooling (TekAI) specifically to give finance teams real-time visibility into batch cost gaps, cashflow forecasts, and inventory KPIs during and after go-live — the kind of operational intelligence that makes the difference between a go-live that “works” and one that actually improves how the business runs from Day 1.

Happy to go deeper on any of these points. What specific module are you struggling with?

— Venkata Siva Reddy Polu, TEKROI
SAP B1 implementation for Hyderabad pharma and manufacturing SMBs

entry.title: Re: Advance Shipment S/4HANA Service Public Cloud
entry.content:
Good day @maurice_broich 

Thank you for your question.

For Public Cloud, the closest standard process is Service Order with Advance Shipment of Spare Parts. You can use this when spare parts need to be shipped in advance, for example:

  • To the customer site before the technician arrives,
  • To the technician before service execution,
  • Or to support field service execution where parts must be available before the work is performed.

However, you must validate the process in your tenant using the available service order item categories and delivery integration. Public Cloud does not allow the same level of IMG based tailoring as Private Cloud, so you must follow the released scope exact configuration steps and logistics behaviour.

In your system, I suggest you check the activated scope and configuration to confirm whether the relevant process is active.

Using Manage Service Orders, create one, enter the sold to party and service object, add a service item if you require, add a spare part item, check whether the spare part item supports advance shipment or delivery relevant processing and save your order. 

Also using Manage Service Orders, open the spare part item, check the item category, plant and storage, quantity and requested date. Check whether logistics follow up or delivery processing is available, then save your order.

Then check outbound delivery and validate the business process.   

See the following Blog and refences for more insights: Serial number management using Cloud Warehouse Management in S/4HANA Cloud

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/adbae5bcd5994f159bf2847a11397b61/8f62c457ace846a2b00df99dd731e6b9.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/adbae5bcd5994f159bf2847a11397b61/0f0d2f82558349b1b4b5ab9465275222.html?locale=en-US

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/s-4-public-cloud-service-ship-spare-part-for-a-service-order/qaq-p/14042872

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: As novas exigências europeias de ESG e reporting corporativo
entry.content:
As empresas em Portugal estão a enfrentar uma nova fase de transformação digital  onde tecnologia, sustentabilidade e talento caminham juntos.

Com as novas exigências europeias de ESG e reporting corporativo, muitas organizações estão a repensar processos, dados e sistemas ERP para responder a desafios cada vez mais complexos.

A SAP em Portugal tem vindo a posicionar-se como uma das principais plataformas para ajudar empresas a responder às novas exigências europeias de ESG (Environmental, Social and Governance) e reporting corporativo, especialmente devido às regras da CSRD (Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive) da União Europeia.

A estratégia da SAP não trata ESG como algo “separado” do negócio. A ideia é integrar sustentabilidade diretamente nos processos empresariais já existentes no ERP.

Por exemplo:

  • emissões de carbono ligadas a compras;
  • consumo energético ligado à produção;
  • indicadores sociais ligados a RH;
  • compliance e auditoria financeira integrados no reporting corporativo.

Isso é muito relevante para empresas portuguesas que já utilizam SAP ERP ou SAP S/4HANA.

Sobre este tema, poderá saber mais no SAP Inside Track Porto. Mais do que nunca, investir na comunidade SAP, na partilha de conhecimento e no desenvolvimento de carreira tornou-se essencial.

É exatamente este espírito que queremos reforçar no SAP Inside Track Porto 2026.

📍No dia 26 de Setembro de 2026, a comunidade SAP volta a reunir-se no Porto para um dia de aprendizagem, networking, inovação e troca de experiências reais entre profissionais, clientes, parceiros, estudantes e entusiastas do ecossistema SAP.

Vamos falar sobre:
✅ESG e reporting europeu
✅Futuro das carreiras SAP
✅SAP Business AI
✅SAP S/4HANA e transformação digital
✅SAP Community
✅Casos reais e tendências do mercado

O SAP Inside Track Porto é mais do que um evento técnico.
É um ponto de encontro para pessoas que acreditam no poder da comunidade para impulsionar conhecimento, oportunidades e inovação.

Ingressos em: https://billetto.pt/e/sap-inside-track-porto-2026-bilhetes-1881735

Nos vemos no Porto! 🚀

entry.title: Need Suggestions for FB60 Mass Upload (Non-PO) – BDC vs BAPI
entry.content:
Need Suggestions for FB60 Mass Upload (Non-PO) – BDC .

Hi Devs

I have a requirement to develop an FB60 mass Non-PO invoice uploader, where invoices are grouped based on the Reference Number.

 

I have already developed a BDC solution, and the posting process is working correctly. However, I'm facing an issue while capturing messages from the BDC execution.

 

The problem is that even when the transaction encounters an error or warning, the returned message type is sometimes 'S' (Success). Currently, I am filtering based on Message Type and Message ID to identify successfully posted documents, but this approach is not reliable in all scenarios.Also there are multiple error in one go.

 

The business users also want to see detailed error information for records that failed during upload. However, many SAP standard error messages are not user-friendly, making it difficult for end users to understand what went wrong and which field caused the issue.

 

A few additional points:

 

- This is a relatively old SAP ECC 6.0 system.

- Before suggesting a BAPI solution, please note that I have already explored BAPI options.

- In my system, automatic tax code calculation is not available.

- The BAPI approach worked only for invoices without tax codes.

- I would also like to avoid manually generating the second line item that FB60 normally creates during manual entry or BDC processing.

 

My questions are:

 

1. How do you reliably identify successful and failed postings in FB60 BDC processing?

2. Is there a better way to capture and present meaningful error messages to users?

3. Has anyone successfully implemented this using a BAPI in an older ECC system, including tax handling?

4. If you have sample code or a working approach (BDC or BAPI), it would be greatly appreciated.

 

Thanks in advance for your suggestions.

entry.title: GRC - Error 500 in the GAE audit review of the FF additional information request workflow
entry.content:
Hello, everyone.

I am reaching out to you because I am encountering an error when running the activity approval workflow for a Firefighter.

The steps I have taken so far are: logging in as the user assigned to the Firefighter, testing the activity, running the Firefighter log synchronization, and seeing the log review request appear in the GRC NWBC.

Up to this point, everything works fine; the controller I have defined can access their inbox, view the request, and request additional information from the user—in this case, regarding the activities they performed with the assigned Firefighter.

The error occurs when the user assigned the Firefighter accesses their inbox to view the request; upon clicking, I receive the following error:

"500 Internal Server Error

Field Symbol has not been assigned yet"

Do you know what might be causing this error?

Within the MSMP flow configuration, I am using the standard settings for the SAP_GRAC_FIREFIGHT_LOG_REPORT process, using GRAC_MSMP_SPM_CNTRL_AGENT as the agent without escalation.

Additionally, the user assigned to the Firefighter has extensive permissions for this test (SAP roles and the SAP_ALL profile).

Thank you for your support. Best regards.

Could be, but where do we draw the line? I've see some great "hits" and some misreble "misses"  with GenAI. The thing is, I was able to see and understand (easily?) what were the hits and misses. Will some AI understand what are the hits and what are the misses of the other AI...

This is more academic, I understand - but the bottom line - in simples terms, we should not exclude human (in the loop?), and we need to work on some guardrails (more?)... My view, at least...

entry.title: Re: Running e-learning based on large files
entry.content:
Hi @BjornR 

From LMS perspective, the maximum size supported by Akamai is 1800 MB (~1.8 GB). For files larger than that, the recommendation is to adjust it by rendering the video in a smaller encoding/resolution, breaking the video into several smaller files, or recreating the content.

  • https://me.sap.com/notes/0002984975
  • https://me.sap.com/notes/0003165280
  • https://me.sap.com/notes/0002318240

Is size is not the issue, the content might not be loading correctly due to something local (browser, internet connection, VPN, etc.). And this needs to be reviewed by the company's IT team.

  • https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2978409
  • https://me.sap.com/notes/2310570
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-learning/implementing-cross-domain-content-delivery/structured-content-troubleshooting

Best regards,

entry.title: System API, Business API, Experience API – as a “governance” concept
entry.content:
Having your APIs into layers is not a new concept[1][2][3][4], but somehow overtime, we may have neglected it. The fact is, we turned design & implementation focus on specific technologies we used, not so much around “who does what”. But, applying API layers may still have many benefits, and it does not mean we need to change the integration technology we use[5].

Here I will try to show some fairly agnostic approach, using some SAP Integration Suite examples, but not limiting ourselves to any specific IPaaS or Event Broker.

To start with…

Yes, ISA-M (Integration Solution Advisory Methodology)[6] does not foster API layers – but is this limiting us in applying this concept with SAP tools?

To be honest no – SAP Integration Suite and its complete ecosystem (IPaaS, API-M, Advanced Event Mesh) are fully flexible set of tools, giving us all the flexibility we need to organize our APIs or EventAPIs the way we want. Publishing APIs via API Business Hub (or subsidiaries) is neither limiting nor contradicting the concept of API layers – this concept is not related with tools, nor even methodology we may use.

As already stated (in my previous article: Integration Guardrails are more than Integration Patterns of the Composable Business), we are defining our Integration Patterns, and Integration Guardrails. At the end it is on us; we define Integration Strategy we will use in the Organization/Enterprise; we define terminology, methodology and frameworks (or guardrails). Now, of course, in some cases it might be good to be very much ridged and apply certain best practices to the letter – but in some cases, we may apply common sense and choose what and how to tailor for our needs. Choosing why and what, how, or which part of what – is not an easy task, but this is why Organizations/Enterprises do have IT Architects. Please note, it’s not exactly “cherry picking”, although sometimes it might look just like that… In fact, tailoring our way is the foundation on which we build our integrations for the Intelligent Enterprise – and we do need to understand the complexity of our Organization/Enterprise, our needs, our aspirations and our maturity. Needless to say, how important it is, we do this right – from the day one…

But to go back to the main topic – yes, we can “add” API/EventAPI layers into our Integration Patterns – and no, this is not tool related (despite some terminology might lead us toward some specific IPaaS, other than SAP).

API layers

We do not “really” build things differently – there is no technical “splitting” or different tools we need to build this concept. This is all about logical split, organizing our APIs and our work per specific layers.

I.e. an Experience API of one Consumer may need to consume data one or more Business APIs, where each Business API may on its own be connected to one or more Providers and its System APIs,

Figure 1. What are API layers?

Logical rules are fairly simple:

  • one Experience APIs is connected to one or more Business API(s)
  • one Business API is connected to one or more System API(s), but in general terms Business API may also be connected to anther Business API(s)

Let’s talk through examples…

We start with simple Sync Request Reply integration pattern.

Figure 2. Simple API layers

What are the layers we have?

  • Provider, as a “system”, is exposing its own API (i.e. OData API), it may be standard or custom, no matter – in all cases we will just call it System API. This is an API provided by the system or application.
  • We will expose this API via API Gateway (i.e. implementing specific policies, throttling etc.), there might be some transformations (or value mappings) in the Cloud Integration before API Gateway – in all cases finally exposed API we will designate as Business API. This is an API we are providing to other Consumers based on the business requirements.
  •  Consumers need to be able to consume data (i.e. invoke HTTP Request, and receive HTTP Reply), and here as well we can have transformations (or value mappings) in the Cloud Integration in between – all this what we consume is called Experience API. This is an API which is adjusted to each Consumer.

Now, these “layers” are not strict technical and technology layers, but rather semantical and logical divisions – and this is critical to understand.

Of course this is a very simple a generic example. Let’s observe now a more realistic example of Sync Request Reply integration pattern, where Consumer(s) are creating new Sales Order in the Provider.

Figure 3. More complex APIs

Now we actually have several Providers:

  • We may have several SAP and non-SAP systems responsible for Order Fulfilment, where different systems are covering different Sales Organizations – i.e. we have non-SAP Provider (RESTful API), legacy SAP Provider (RFC call) and S/4HANA Provider (OData API). Each underlying system is exposing its own System API (although RFC is not “strictly” speaking an API, but we will be consuming it through Cloud Integration adapter).
  • Despite the fact Order Fulfilment is managed by different systems for different Sales Organizations, we want to provide seamless experience for all connecting systems when consuming this Business API. We will ensure routing policy within API Gateway, as well as necessary transformations within Cloud Integration – i.e. to transform message for non-SAP Provider or transform for legacy SAP Provider. From the perspective of consuming systems, this complexity is hidden – they will see only single API schema exposed to them.
  • Finally, each Consumer will consume data through its own Experience API. Please note, Experience API may be connected to several Business APIs (even though in this diagram only one Business API is shown).

What about EDA and EventAPIs?

But this concept does not end with “standard” Sync Request Reply integration pattern APIs. Yes, we can use it with PubSub or Async Request-Reply as well.

Figure 4. EventAPI layers

This example is with PubSub Integration Pattern.

  • Provider is publishing a business event to subscribed Consumer(s). In case of S/4HANA it can be standard CloudEvent, or RAP created event, or we may use Advance Event Mesh add-on (ASAPIO) for building and publishing events – in all cases we call this System EventAPI. This is an EventAPI provided by the system or application.
  • An event message is published directly on the Advance Event Mesh Topic, or optionally it may go through Cloud Integration (i.e. message payload adjustment). The Topic with its Schema Registry and any associated transformation (either in Cloud Integration or MI – Micro Integrations) is now representing Business EventAPI. Why Topic, why not Queue? Simple because Queue is actually a subscriber to the Topic, and it may subscribe only to subset of Topic messages (i.e. using dynamic elements) or it may subscribe to several Topics or both. It is the Topic which is providing EventAPI to other Consumers based on the business requirements.
  • Consumers may be subscribed directly to the Topic, or it may consume data through Queue, and in some cases additional transformations are needed between Queue and the Consumer – all this what we consume is called Experience EventAPI. This is now an EventAPI which is filtered and/or adjusted to each Consumer.

The proposed concept is “splitting” Advance Event Mesh broker into two layers –part which belongs to Business EventAPI and part which belongs to Experience EventAPI. But rational is, I believe, quite strong – it is Topic and Queue what is decoupling Provider and Consumer, so it does make sense to assign broker operations into both layers – well, if we fully embrace this approach…

Worth noticing, with some other brokers this “splitting” may not work exactly like this. If we use Kafka broker we are using persisting Topics with multiple copies. But this is still not an issue. Applying the similar logic, each copy may be considered as part of the Experience EventAPI, right?

What about complex scenarios?  

In reality, integration flows are usually more complex. In this following example we will see that one Business API may invoke another Business API, In this example we will also see Experience API may be connected to more than one Business API.

Figure 5. Complex scenario with APIs and EventAPIs

In many cases we will have to combine APIs and EventAPIs. Here is one possible “complex” scenario:

  • Provider is publishing a business event. This is System EventAPI.
  • But this event might not have all necessary message data we need; it may serve only as a trigger to Cloud Integration for composing more complex message for the Business EventAPI. Cloud Integration will be requesting additional data from the other system through respective Business API and System API, and then publish full message to the Advanced Event Mesh Topic.
  • Also, the Consumer which is subscribed (either directly or via Queue) may expect further enhanced message data as part of its Experience EventAPI. Again, Cloud Integration will be acquiring additional data from the other system through respective Business API and System API.

In this example I did not cover all the possible complexities, as this is only a high-level concept document– but I believe the “picture” is clear…

Other Integration Patterns…

The same way we can do this concept with Sync Request-Reply, Async Request-Replay and PubSub integration patterns – no limitations to apply it with other integration patterns, as long as we have “things” decoupled or loosely coupled. Will not go into details, at least not in this article, as I believe “picture” is clear here as well…

It’s all about reusability, governance…

Why? Why should we introduce this concept? It’s not that we did not use all this in past, but we just did not make any “logical” separations…

I am not bringing any new technology or solution, I am talking about the concept – and now I need to explain its main benefits, otherwise, why should we bother to do it, right?

Reusability

From one side. we work in heterogeneous environments; connecting various Applications and Data Objects is usually not very straight forward activity. Using middleware is almost must, if we want to ensure some sort of decoupled or loosely coupled integrations (needed for the Intelligent Enterprise).

From the other side, we want to have our integrations managed, catalogued, and ready to be re-used by other Applications.

  • The thing is, each Provider has either standard or custom APIs (or EventAPIs). These System APIs (or EventAPIs) are tailored by the Provider system and its underlaying technology – and usually does not include Business needs or non-functional requirements (i.e. policies) of the particular Organization/Enterprise.
  • To build reusable APIs within Organization/Enterprise, we may have to take those System APIs, enhance it, transform it, or even group it into logical units which will be then exposed for further (re)use. By doing that, we are in fact building Business APIs (or EventAPIs).
  • Finally, we do build Business APIs to enable maximum reusability within the Organization/Enterprise, but in some cases, some Consumer(s) may not (technically) be able to consume those APIs without further adjusting to its own technology – so there might be an additional layer on top of Business APIs preparing data for the particular Consumer(s). This is now called Experience APIs (or EventAPIs)

Clearly, reusability is achieved by Business API, as here we build APIs which will meet the majority (if not all) Business needs and non-functional requirements of our Organization/Enterprise. We are fully decoupling Provider and Consumer(s), we do not adjust Provider toward each specific Consumer(s), nor do we adjust Consumer(s) to accept data from each Provider. Yet, in all cases we do have fully managed Business APIs (or EventAPIs) ready for every new Consumer to (re)use.

Governance

With Business APIs, we are bridging the complexity between Provider and Consumer(s), it’s built to be reused, but it’s built to be governed as well.

  • Provider is responsible for exposing its System APIs providing all necessary Business Data Objects and relevant statuses as per adjected processes. Provider is (normally) not aware how this data will be consumed by other Consumer(s).
  • Middleware is responsible for consuming System APIs, transforming it (per general need) and exposing it as Business APIs as reusable integration artifacts. Middleware is aware of the schemes and definitions of all relevant System APIs coming from Provider, as well as requirements for building general-purpose Business APIs ready for Consumer(s) to take and consume.
  • Consumer(s) are responsible for performing any individual adjustments and build for itself Experience APIs, for its own Business Data Objects and relevant statuses. In this process we may also need to use specific middleware components/tools. Consumer(s) are not aware how this data was provided by the Provider.

Figure 6. Split of responsibilities

By having this split, we can now easily “play” with specific governance rules and set rules of engagements of different Agile teams. I.e.:

  • Product Team, working on the specific Provider system, is responsible for exposing relevant data via System APIs, taking into consideration requirements of the overall Business Process. However, this team is not involved in the overall integration design process.
  • Integration Team, working on various middleware tools, is responsible for understanding both technology (i.e. System APIs technical details), but also overall requirements for the integration (i.e. what is it used for, by whom, how etc.). Obviously in its work Integration Team will apply specific Integration Guardrails and Guidelines. While it might sound a bit strange (to some), this is not only technical tasks but usually requires understanding of the underlaying Business Process. One does not have to be an expert at all, but knowing how to ask right questions is critical. This is why Integration Architecture cannot be just “coding” and “development” in middleware tools. The role of the Integration Architects is critical in the overall integration process, as they are solutioning this “bridge” between Provider and Consumer.
  • Product Team, working on the specific Consumer system, is responsible for building relevant Experience APIs, taking into consideration requirements and (possible?) constraints of its Application. However, this team is not involved in the overall integration design process. It is interesting to note here, that this Product Team may also using different integration IT Components (or even IPaaS) which are more tightly connected with the consuming Application (i.e. using Azure Service Bus with Azure Function for inbound/outbound traffic to/from Dynamics 365 might make perfect sense).

Figure 7. Move toward API layers

There are several (positive!) implications from this split of Roles & Responsibilities.

  1. Integration is everybody business – integrating two or more Applications is not a sole responsibility of the Integration Team.
  2. Governance is everybody concern – all participants must do their part as per prescribed governance rules of engagements, we all must “give” something in order to “get” something.
  3. Integration Architects design integration – people who have knowledge and skills are entrusted to do what they are meant to be doing.

As we know from experiences, “dropping” integration requirements or designs form the Product Owners or “other” IT Architects, was usually resulting in poorly governed integration flows, usually built to fit one purpose. We cannot expect Integration Governance to be enforced by people whose immediate interest is not related with integrations. No hard feelings, but how it would look like if one Product Team would be designing Business Processes in the other Product?

Integration design for “reusable” Business APIs, and ideally only Business APIs (as designing anything on the Provider system and Consumer experience is not their responsibility), has to be assigned to Integration Architects and Integration Team.

Conclusion

The main purpose of this article is to:

  • Demonstrate how easily we can apply API layers with SAP Integration Sute.
  • Explain what the key benefits are of “breaking” APIs into layers.
  • (Re)emphasize the need for proper Integration Governance and the role of Integration Architects.

This article is (obviously) not a technical article. Here are I am discussing about a concept which may further enhance our Decoupled/Lousily-coupled integrations – and make our journey toward Composable Business/Application Architecture and Intelligence Enterprise much easier.

It is for each Organization/Enterprise to evaluate what is their path. General view is, complex Organizations/Enterprises with very heterogenic environments might see numerous benefits in bringing additional governance with API layers, in their organizational schema.

But, as always (in IT), one size does not fit all…

Acknowledgment

*) Intro photo by Conny Schneider on Unsplash

References

[1] API Handyman Blog: API Handyman | Organizing APIs in System, Business, and Experience Layers

[2] API Handyman Blog: API Handyman | Organize APIs around business domains and capabilities, not tools

[3] Medium Blog: API-Led Principles: What’s Permitted and What’s Not | Medium

[4] LinkedIn Article: Distinction between system-, process and experience APIs

[5] MuleSoft: Types of APIs and how to determine which to build | Mulesoft

[6] SAP: Integration Solution Advisory Methodology (ISA-M):... - SAP Community

entry.title: How to bypass BI launchpad logon page in BO 2025
entry.content:
I'm having trouble migrating my company's login page, which bypassed login in versions 4.3 and earlier, to version 2025.

Previously, I used several JAR files, and I managed to migrate to using a REST web service to perform login and retrieve the token. In the CMC, I can see the session of the logged-in user. However, when I try to redirect the JSP to /BOE/BI, a new login is requested.

The same behavior is not observed when opening a document with a previously logged-in user (Opendocument.jsp...).

Excerpt from my code.

<%@page import="br.gov.senado.bo.rest.BORestClient"%>
<%@page contentType="text/html;charset=UTF-8" language="java"
        import="java.io.*,
                java.net.*,
                java.net.http.*,
                java.net.http.HttpClient,
                java.net.http.HttpRequest,
                java.net.http.HttpResponse,
                java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets,
                java.time.Duration,
                java.util.*" %>
<%!
/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   boLogin.jsp — SAP BusinessObjects 4.4 patch 800
   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   ESTRATÉGIA: Server-to-Server com propagação de cookie
   ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

   FLUXO COMPLETO (sem nenhum POST ou token visível ao browser):

   1. Browser faz GET/POST em boLogin.jsp com usuário+senha
   2. JSP chama POST /biprws/logon/long  →  obtém logonToken  (server-side)
   3. JSP chama GET  /biprws/session     →  troca token por cookies reais
      de sessão do Tomcat (JSESSIONID, BOID, sap-usercontext)
      usando HttpClient com CookieManager compartilhado
   4. JSP escreve os cookies recebidos no response do próprio browser
      via response.addCookie()  (Set-Cookie ao browser)
   5. JSP faz response.sendRedirect("/BOE/BI")
      Agora o browser JÁ TEM os cookies do Tomcat do BO → acesso direto
      sem nova solicitação de login

   Por que isso funciona:
   • O token NUNCA trafega pelo browser
   • O browser recebe os cookies de sessão reais do BO Tomcat
   • O sendRedirect envia um GET para /BOE/BI — o Tomcat encontra a sessão
     pelos cookies e abre o Launch Pad diretamente

   Pré-requisito:
   • Esta JSP deve estar hospedada no MESMO domínio/host que o BO
     (ou o domínio dos cookies deve ser configurado explicitamente abaixo)
   • Se estiver em domínio diferente: use proxy reverso nginx na mesma origem
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

    // ── Configuração ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    static final String BO_BASE_URL    = "http://server.com";   // sem barra final
    static final String BO_AUTH_TYPE   = "secEnterprise";
    static final String BO_LAUNCHPAD   = "/BOE/BI";
    static final String REST_LOGON     = "/biprws/logon/long";
    static final String REST_SESSION   = "/biprws/session";         // endpoint de sessão
    static final int    TIMEOUT_SEC    = 15;
    // Cookies que o Tomcat do BO usa para manter sessão — propague todos
    static final List<String> SESSION_COOKIES = List.of(
        "JSESSIONID", "BOID", "sap-usercontext", "SAP_SESSIONID"
    );
    // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    // CookieManager compartilhado: captura todos os Set-Cookie do servidor BO
    static final CookieManager cookieManager = new CookieManager(
        null, CookiePolicy.ACCEPT_ALL
    );

    static final HttpClient httpClient = HttpClient.newBuilder()
        .connectTimeout(Duration.ofSeconds(TIMEOUT_SEC))
        .followRedirects(HttpClient.Redirect.NORMAL)
        .cookieHandler(cookieManager)           // ← captura cookies automaticamente
        .build();

    // ── Passo 1: Autenticar e obter logonToken ────────────────────────────────
    static String obterLogonToken(String usuario, String senha, String tipoAuth)
            throws Exception {

    	System.out.println("boLogin.jsp - obterLogonToken - Inicio");
    	
    	String token = BORestClient.logonRestFul(usuario, senha);
    	
    	System.out.println("boLogin.jsp - obterLogonToken - token: " + token);
    	
        String endpoint = BO_BASE_URL + REST_LOGON;
        String body     = String.format(
            "{\"userName\":\"%s\",\"password\":\"%s\",\"auth\":\"%s\",\"clientType\":\"webBrowser\"}",
            escJson(usuario), escJson(senha), escJson(tipoAuth)
        );

        HttpRequest req = HttpRequest.newBuilder()
            .uri(URI.create(endpoint))
            .timeout(Duration.ofSeconds(TIMEOUT_SEC))
            .header("Content-Type", "application/json")
            .header("Accept",       "application/json")
            .POST(HttpRequest.BodyPublishers.ofString(body, StandardCharsets.UTF_8))
            .build();

        HttpResponse<String> resp = httpClient.send(
            req, HttpResponse.BodyHandlers.ofString(StandardCharsets.UTF_8));

        if (resp.statusCode() < 200 || resp.statusCode() >= 300) {
            String msg = extrairCampoJson(resp.body(), "message");
            if (msg == null) msg = extrairCampoJson(resp.body(), "error_message");
            if (msg == null) msg = "HTTP " + resp.statusCode();
            throw new RuntimeException("Falha na autenticação: " + msg);
        }

        String token2 = extrairCampoJson(resp.body(), "logonToken");
        
    	System.out.println("boLogin.jsp - obterLogonToken - token2: " + token2);
    	
        if (token2 == null || token.isEmpty())
            throw new RuntimeException("Token não retornado pelo servidor BO.");

        return token2;
    }

    // ── Passo 2: Trocar token por cookies de sessão reais ────────────────────
    // Chama GET /biprws/session com o X-SAP-LogonToken no header.
    // O Tomcat do BO processa o token e devolve Set-Cookie com JSESSIONID/BOID.
    // O cookieManager captura esses cookies automaticamente.
    static void trocarTokenPorCookies(String logonToken) throws Exception {
        String endpoint = BO_BASE_URL + REST_SESSION;

        HttpRequest req = HttpRequest.newBuilder()
            .uri(URI.create(endpoint))
            .timeout(Duration.ofSeconds(TIMEOUT_SEC))
            .header("Accept",              "application/json")
            .header("X-SAP-LogonToken",    "\"" + logonToken + "\"")  // aspas obrigatórias
            .GET()
            .build();

        HttpResponse<String> resp = httpClient.send(
            req, HttpResponse.BodyHandlers.ofString(StandardCharsets.UTF_8));

        // 200 ou 304 são aceitos; qualquer 4xx/5xx é erro
        if (resp.statusCode() >= 400)
            throw new RuntimeException(
                "Erro ao validar sessão no BO: HTTP " + resp.statusCode());
    }

    // ── Passo 3: Propagar cookies para o browser ──────────────────────────────
    // Recupera do CookieManager os cookies que o Tomcat do BO setou e os
    // escreve no response HTTP para o browser do usuário.
    static void propagarCookiesParaBrowser(HttpServletResponse browserResp,
                                           String dominio) throws Exception {
        URI uri = URI.create(BO_BASE_URL + BO_LAUNCHPAD);
        List<HttpCookie> cookies = cookieManager.getCookieStore().get(uri);

        // também pega cookies sem path específico
        List<HttpCookie> todos = cookieManager.getCookieStore().getCookies();

        Set<String> adicionados = new HashSet<>();

        for (HttpCookie hc : todos) {
            String nome = hc.getName();

            // filtra apenas os cookies de sessão relevantes do BO
            boolean eRelevante = SESSION_COOKIES.stream()
                .anyMatch(n -> nome.equalsIgnoreCase(n) || nome.startsWith("SAP"));

            if (!eRelevante || adicionados.contains(nome)) continue;

            Cookie c = new Cookie(nome, hc.getValue());
            c.setPath(hc.getPath() != null ? hc.getPath() : "/");
            c.setHttpOnly(true);
            c.setSecure(BO_BASE_URL.startsWith("https"));
            if (hc.getMaxAge() > 0)
                c.setMaxAge((int) hc.getMaxAge());
            // domínio: só setar se for necessário (mesmo host = não precisa)
            // c.setDomain(dominio);

            browserResp.addCookie(c);
            adicionados.add(nome);
        }
    }

    // ── Utilitários ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

    static String extrairCampoJson(String json, String campo) {
        if (json == null) return null;
        String key = "\"" + campo + "\"";
        int idx = json.indexOf(key);
        if (idx < 0) return null;
        int colon = json.indexOf(':', idx + key.length());
        if (colon < 0) return null;
        // valor pode ser string ou número
        int start = colon + 1;
        while (start < json.length() && json.charAt(start) == ' ') start++;
        if (start >= json.length()) return null;
        if (json.charAt(start) == '"') {
            // string: encontra fechamento de aspas (ignora escaped)
            int end = start + 1;
            while (end < json.length()) {
                if (json.charAt(end) == '"' && json.charAt(end-1) != '\\') break;
                end++;
            }
            return json.substring(start + 1, end);
        } else {
            // número/booleano: até próxima vírgula ou }
            int end = start;
            while (end < json.length() &&
                   json.charAt(end) != ',' &&
                   json.charAt(end) != '}') end++;
            return json.substring(start, end).trim();
        }
    }

    static String escJson(String s) {
        if (s == null) return "";
        return s.replace("\\", "\\\\")
                .replace("\"", "\\\"")
                .replace("\n", "\\n")
                .replace("\r", "\\r")
                .replace("\t", "\\t");
    }

    static String escHtml(String s) {
        if (s == null) return "";
        return s.replace("&","&").replace("<","<")
                .replace(">",">").replace("\"",""").replace("'","'");
    }
%>
<%
/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   Lógica principal da JSP
   ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */

String metodo  = request.getMethod();
String usuario = request.getParameter("username");
String senha   = request.getParameter("password");
String tipoAuth= request.getParameter("authType");
String errMsg  = null;

if (tipoAuth == null || tipoAuth.isEmpty()) tipoAuth = BO_AUTH_TYPE;

System.out.println("boLogin.jsp - metodo: "+metodo);
System.out.println("boLogin.jsp - usuario: "+usuario);
System.out.println("boLogin.jsp - senha: "+ senha);
System.out.println("boLogin.jsp - authType: "+tipoAuth);

// ── Processar login (POST) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
if ("POST".equalsIgnoreCase(metodo) &&
     usuario != null && !usuario.trim().isEmpty() &&
     senha   != null && !senha.isEmpty()) {

    try {
        // 1. Obtém logonToken (server → BO, token nunca vai ao browser)
        String logonToken = obterLogonToken(
            usuario.trim(), senha, tipoAuth.trim());

        System.out.println("boLogin.jsp - logonToken: "+logonToken);

        // 2. Valida token no endpoint de sessão → Tomcat seta cookies reais
        //    O CookieManager captura JSESSIONID, BOID, sap-usercontext
        trocarTokenPorCookies(logonToken);

        // 3. Copia os cookies de sessão do BO para o response do browser
        //    A partir daqui o browser tem os cookies válidos do Tomcat BO
        propagarCookiesParaBrowser(response, request.getServerName());

        // 4. sendRedirect simples para /BOE/BI
        //    Funciona porque o browser AGORA TEM os cookies de sessão do BO
        //    O Tomcat encontra a sessão pelos cookies → abre Launch Pad direto
        response.sendRedirect(BO_BASE_URL + BO_LAUNCHPAD);
        return;  // encerra a JSP — nada mais é enviado ao browser

    } catch (Exception e) {
        errMsg = e.getMessage();
        // loga o erro no servidor (não expõe detalhes ao browser)
        application.log("[boLogin.jsp] Erro de autenticação para [" +
            escHtml(usuario) + "]: " + e.getMessage());
    }
} else if ("POST".equalsIgnoreCase(metodo)) {
    errMsg = "Usuário e senha são obrigatórios.";
}

// ── Se chegou aqui: exibe formulário (GET ou erro) ────────────────────────
%><!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="pt-BR">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8"/>
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1"/>
  <title>SAP BusinessObjects — Login</title>
  <style>
    *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0 }

    body {
      font-family: "Segoe UI", Arial, sans-serif;
      background: #f0f2f5;
      min-height: 100vh;
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      justify-content: center;
      color: #1a1a2e;
    }

    .card {
      background: #fff;
      border-radius: 6px;
      padding: 2.6rem 2.2rem 2.2rem;
      width: 380px;
      box-shadow: 0 2px 20px rgba(0,0,0,.13);
      border-top: 4px solid #0070d2;
    }

    .logo {
      display: flex;
      align-items: center;
      gap: .7rem;
      margin-bottom: 1.8rem;
    }

    .logo-box {
      width: 38px; height: 38px;
      background: #0070d2;
      border-radius: 6px;
      display: grid;
      grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
      gap: 3px;
      padding: 7px;
    }

    .logo-box span {
      background: #fff;
      border-radius: 1px;
    }

    .logo-text strong  { display: block; font-size: 1rem; color: #1a1a2e }
    .logo-text small   { font-size: .75rem; color: #888; font-weight: 400 }

    .field       { margin-bottom: 1.1rem }
    label        { display:block; font-size:.77rem; font-weight:600;
                   color:#555; margin-bottom:.35rem; letter-spacing:.03em }

    input[type="text"],
    input[type="password"],
    select {
      width: 100%;
      padding: .68rem .85rem;
      border: 1px solid #cdd5e0;
      border-radius: 4px;
      font-size: .92rem;
      color: #1a1a2e;
      transition: border-color .2s, box-shadow .2s;
      outline: none;
      background: #fafbfc;
    }

    input:focus, select:focus {
      border-color: #0070d2;
      box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(0,112,210,.15);
      background: #fff;
    }

    select { cursor: pointer }

    .btn {
      width: 100%;
      padding: .78rem;
      background: #0070d2;
      color: #fff;
      border: none;
      border-radius: 4px;
      font-size: .97rem;
      font-weight: 600;
      cursor: pointer;
      margin-top: .6rem;
      letter-spacing: .02em;
      transition: background .2s;
    }

    .btn:hover    { background: #005ab3 }
    .btn:active   { background: #004a99 }
    .btn:disabled { opacity: .6; cursor: not-allowed }

    .alert {
      background: #fff0f1;
      border-left: 4px solid #e63946;
      color: #b71c2e;
      padding: .75rem 1rem;
      border-radius: 4px;
      font-size: .87rem;
      margin-bottom: 1.2rem;
      line-height: 1.4;
    }

    .footer {
      margin-top: 1.6rem;
      padding-top: 1rem;
      border-top: 1px solid #eef0f4;
      font-size: .72rem;
      color: #aaa;
      text-align: center;
      letter-spacing: .03em;
    }
  </style>
</head>
<body>

<div class="card">

  <div class="logo">
    <div class="logo-box">
      <span></span><span></span><span></span><span></span>
    </div>
    <div class="logo-text">
      <strong>SAP BusinessObjects</strong>
      <small>BI Launch Pad · 4.4 patch 800</small>
    </div>
  </div>

  <% if (errMsg != null) { %>
  <div class="alert"><%=escHtml(errMsg)%></div>
  <% } %>

  <%-- 
    Formulário POST para esta mesma JSP.
    O servidor processa, propaga cookies e faz sendRedirect internamente.
    O browser NUNCA recebe nem vê o logonToken.
  --%>
  <form method="POST" action="boLogin.jsp" autocomplete="on"
        onsubmit="this.querySelector('.btn').disabled=true;
                  this.querySelector('.btn').textContent='Autenticando…'">

    <div class="field">
      <label for="username">Usuário</label>
      <input type="text" id="username" name="username"
             value="<%=escHtml(usuario != null ? usuario : "")%>"
             placeholder="nome de usuário SAP BO"
             autocomplete="username" required autofocus/>
    </div>

    <div class="field">
      <label for="password">Senha</label>
      <input type="password" id="password" name="password"
             placeholder="••••••••"
             autocomplete="current-password" required/>
    </div>

    <div class="field">
      <label for="authType">Tipo de autenticação</label>
      <select id="authType" name="authType">
        <option value="secEnterprise"
          <%="secEnterprise".equals(tipoAuth)?"selected":""%>>
          secEnterprise (BO nativo)</option>
        <option value="secLDAP"
          <%="secLDAP".equals(tipoAuth)?"selected":""%>>
          secLDAP (Active Directory)</option>
        <option value="secSAPR3"
          <%="secSAPR3".equals(tipoAuth)?"selected":""%>>
          secSAPR3 (SAP R/3)</option>
        <option value="secWinAD"
          <%="secWinAD".equals(tipoAuth)?"selected":""%>>
          secWinAD (Windows AD)</option>
      </select>
    </div>

    <button type="submit" class="btn">Entrar no BI Launch Pad</button>
  </form>

  <div class="footer">SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise © SAP SE</div>
</div>

</body>
</html>

entry.title: New MSO version 2604 build 199929.20172

entry.title: Re: Missing Assign Button in SSAM v2405
entry.content: Just faced the same issue. It looks like that button appears only if the responsible person is  assigned to the maintanance order. We use 2410 version

entry.title: Re: How I Connected Claude AI to My SAP ABAP System Using MCP — A Complete Windows Guide
entry.content:
Hola. En este blog utilizan la conexión http (icm) para conectarse a SAP. Tambien se puede realizar la conexion directamente al sistema, pero necesitas otros requisitos para la correcta configuración de MCP, por ejemplo, necesitas el SAP NWRFC SDK  para permitir la conexión directa, incluyendo SAPROUTER. Puedes guiarte con el siguiente ejemplo: https://github.com/domatwonderful/sap-ecc-rfc-prototype

En el punto 3 del ejemplo, pasan los datos de conexión, pero no  esta el saprouter, ese parámetro es

"SAP_ROUTER": "/H/hostname_o_IP_saprouter/S/puerto/H/hostname_servidor_SAP/S/puerto"

Espero te sirva. A mi me funciono correctamente con Claude.

entry.title: Re: EPPM Business Requirements , S4 Hana Private cloud
entry.content: SAP Sales Cloud (CRM system) would be better suited to your requirements.  Once you are moving forward with a detailed quote then CPM can come into play with the Bid Structure Financial Plan.

entry.title: Re: Stay Certified Process Clarification and advice for all current SAP FI and CO certifications
entry.content: You have the opportunity now to try our practical examination. I hope you find the experience fun.

entry.title: Brave CFO Gathering 2026 — що обговорюватимуть фінансові лідери у 2026
entry.content:
5 червня у Києві та онлайн відбудеться Brave CFO Gathering 2026
— початок сезону XXIV Ukrainian CFO Forum, який об’єднає фінансових директорів, CEO, власників бізнесу та інвесторів для обговорення ключових викликів і можливостей сучасного бізнесу.

У центрі дискусій цього року:

  • трансформація ролі CFO — від фінансової звітності до управління стійкістю та зростанням компанії;
  • використання штучного інтелекту у фінансах та автоматизації бізнес-процесів;
  • ERP-трансформація та перехід на сучасні системи управління підприємством;
  • управління ризиками та Due Diligence;
  • доступ до капіталу та фінансування від міжнародних фінансових інституцій;
  • масштабування бізнесу в умовах нової економічної реальності.

Деталі:

🕛Коли: 5 червня 2026 року
🌍Де: Київ + онлайн

Реєстрація: за посиланням

Діє спеціальна знижка 20% на всі формати участі за промокодом партнера BraveBE

Програма та реєстрація:https://cfo.ua/cfoforum/

Команда SAP Ukraine також відвідає Brave CFO Gathering 2026.
Будемо раді зустрітися з колегами, клієнтами та партнерами, обговорити актуальні виклики цифрової трансформації, ERP-проєкти, використання AI у фінансах та сучасні підходи до управління бізнесом.

entry.title: Re: Synchronous Goods Receipts in EWM
entry.content:
@pabhijit : It's not possible in the synchronous process to consolidate the line item quantities and pack into one handling unit. However, you receive all the line items to same bin and pack using either /SCWM/PACK Fiori App or Warehouse Stock Fiori App to achieve your solution. It will be two steps for the user.

Step 1: Receive as loose quantities to the same bin all the line items in the PO.

Step 2: Pack using above Fiori Apps with the respective packaging material.

Regards

Sunil

entry.title: Re: Missing Assign Button in SSAM v2405
entry.content: Found a root cause . The app checks if the role VW is assigned for the workorder. It's done in the IsAssignEnableWorkOrder.js export default function IsAssignEnableWorkOrder(context, actionBinding) { const binding = actionBinding || context.binding; return isSupervisorSectionVisibleForWO(context).then(function(visible) { if (!visible) { return false; } return context.read('/SAPAssetManager/Services/AssetManager.service', binding['@odata.readLink'] + '/WOPartners', [], "$filter=PartnerFunction eq 'VW'") .then(partners => !ValidationLibrary.evalIsEmpty(partners)); }); }

entry.title: Re: Automatic Warehouse Task Creation at GR Flag not working
entry.content:
HI @RashmiBhandari_1999-1 

This field is only used when posting a Goods Receipt (GR) in MIGO for Synchronous Goods Receipt. It automatically creates a warehouse task (WT) based on the warehouse process type (WPT) maintained in SPRO under: EWM -> Interfaces -> Goods Movement -> Make Settings for Synchronous Goods Movement Posting.

Regards

Sunil

entry.title: Supplier Down payment adjustments and tracking
entry.content:
Hi members,

I am using SAP S4 HANA Public cloud. We receive multiple proforma invoices for advances from suppliers. Each advance payment is a loan/borrowing from a bank. We are required to trace the geneology of each proforma invoice from the date and amount of advance payment -> actual date and amount of supplier invoice and corresponding adjustment against down payment -> date of sale of this product to the end customer. Is there any app that helps track the purchasing side from  advance payment to adjustment against supplier invoice?

entry.title: Netweaver and BW compatibility with BODS 2025
entry.content:
BODS DIGITS is compatible with SAP applications that run on supported SAP NetWeaver versions, with 7.5 SP31 being the latest certified version. We are currently planning to upgrade to BODS DIGITS, using SBOP_BI DIGITS as the underlying platform. However, our ECC system is running on SAP NetWeaver 7.5 SP28.

1) Could you please confirm whether BODS DIGITS (with SBOP_BI DIGITS as the underlying platform) will work with ECC system (SAP NetWeaver 7.5 SP28)? 

2)Also  Could you please confirm whether BODS DIGITS (with SBOP_BI DIGITS as the underlying platform) will work with BW system (750 SP28) 


--- Support Assistant ---
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: Data Services install \ upgrade

Q: What version of Data Services are you installing?
A: Data services DIGITS

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Netweaver and BW compatibility with BODS 2025
entry.content:
Dear customer,

 

Please check DS 2025 PAM document https://support.sap.com/content/dam/launchpad/en_us/pam/pam-essentials/TIP/PAM_DataServices_2025.pdf page 20. 

The last certify version is SAP NetWeaver 7.5 SP31 and SAP BW 7.5 SP31

The annotation says "** by backward compatibility, versions within a minor version are compatible unless specified. "

So SAP NetWeaver 7.5 SP28 and SAP BW 7.5 SP28 are supported

 

Best regards,

Chester

entry.title: Re: SAP Enable Now - Translation function stopped working
entry.content:
Hi Melissa,

There may be an issue with the translation functionality but it your issue could also be related to the way that Producer determines where to place the translated content.  Prior to starting the translation, you must add a Text Unit of type Translation Information to the place in the content structure where the translated content will be placed.  If you have left existing Text Units of type Translation Information in your content structure, the translation may well be occurring and the output placed into some other part of the content hierarchy.  Prior to starting the process, please ensure that all existing Text Units that are not a part of the current translation process have been deleted.  If this is not the issue, please raise a support ticket using Component ID KM-SEN-PRO and have support take a closer look.

Regards
Shane
